content.boundary: Applies to primary education structure only (grades 1-8 span); does not restructure secondary education, and requires village settlements to be within a commutable distance of a town-based consolidation site.
content.change_lever: National curricular/structural reorganization establishing a two-tier primary model: grades 1-4 (or 1-6 in 8-year systems) retained in village buildings; grades 5-8 (or later stage) consolidated into a designated ISCED2-type school in a nearby town, following the shortened-primary-plus-centralized-later-stage template.
content.name: Structural grade split: village-based lower grades with centrally sited upper-grade schools
content.system_problem: Village schools facing enrollment decline currently face a binary choice between full retention (increasingly fiscally unviable given national per-pupil funding formulas and minimum class-size thresholds) or full closure (severing the community hub and imposing long commutes on young children); no intermediate structural option separates the local-proximity needs of young children from the resource/curriculum-breadth needs of older pupils.
